relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job answered again.
He called them miserable comforters.
Their words were wind without end.
If roles were reversed, Job could also bury them in words.
But he would rather strengthen and restrain grief.
Speech does not remove his pain, and silence does not release it.
He feels worn out and desolated.
His body itself has become witness against him.
He experiences God’s anger as tearing opposition.
People gather in reproach and strike at him.
He feels handed over to the unjust.
Ease was shattered, and Job became a target.
Pain surrounds him like archers.
Breach follows breach.
He wears sackcloth and sinks his dignity into dust.
His face is marked by weeping and shadow.
Yet his hands are not violent, and his prayer is clean.
He asks earth not to cover his blood or silence his cry.
Even now, he trusts there is a witness in heaven.
Friends mock while his eyes pour tears toward the Divine.
He longs for someone to argue for a human before God.
The path ahead feels short and without return.
